About

Sharp ideas.
Honest prose.

I'm a writer from Mount Carmel, Illinois — a place that gave me plenty to think against. My work sits at the crossing of institutional critique, theological inquiry, and the politics of everyday life.

I write because language is the last thing that can't be surveilled — and because most essays are too comfortable with the institutions they describe. Mine aren't.

In the fall I'll be studying mathematics at Harvard College as a QuestBridge College Match Scholar.

Drafts before anything leaves my desk
Writing

A collection of essays, criticism, and longform work. Each piece is an attempt to see clearly — and to say it plainly.

Critical Essay · Gold Medal

The Theology
of Surveillance

A research-driven essay arguing how high-control religion turns omniscience into policing, and why punitive theology is culturally conditioned, not divinely necessary.

★ Scholastic Gold Medal
↗
Personal Essay · Gold Key

Denim Gospel

A memoir of apostasy, sparked by American Eagle and the first question prayer couldn't answer.

★ Scholastic Gold Key
↗
Writing Portfolio · Silver Medal

Eden,
Surveilled

This portfolio is an autopsy of a theology that tried to script my inner life. Each piece approaches the same wound—the experience of being endlessly watched by God, church, and community—from a different angle, until the gaze finally cracks and I begin to see myself without a spotlight.

◆ Scholastic Silver Medal
↗
"
Writing that refuses to flinch at what it finds — that is the only kind worth doing.
— Aiden McBroom
